rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE FIORANI LIVING TRUST DTD. 1/25/02 TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For An Order Directing The Receiver To Distribute Proceeds Of Sale In Conformity With Settlement Agreement, Including Attorneys Fees And Costs, To Confirm Modification Of The Settlement Agreement, And For A Finding That Ronald Mazzaferro Has No Further Interest In The Fiorani Living Trust; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday March 27, 2019 line 1. PETITIONER WILLIAM PARISI Notice Of Motion And Motion For An Order Directing The Receiver To Distribute Proceeds Of Sale In Conformity With Settlement Agreement, Including Attorneys Fees And Costs, To Confirm Modification Of The Settlement Agreement, And For A Finding That Ronald Mazzaferro Has No Further Interest In The Fiorani Living Trust; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities Hearing Required. In order to make a determination as to the compensation and in light of counsels' alleged agreement to abate fees in order to ensure that the beneficiary received at least $500,000, the Court needs information regarding the costs of winding up the receivership, i.e. reserve. Previously the court issued a tentative ruling requesting that everyone involved submit their fees and costs claims. The Court continued the hearing with instructions to do so. The Court received several claims from parties and their counsel. No claim was received from the receiver and his counsel. The Court received a response from the Receiver indicating that no fees are sought "at this time." The Court interprets this statement to mean that receiver and receiver's counsel are not seeking any fees in this action because the Court ordered that claims for fees incurred (and estimated, if applicable) are to be submitted in advance of this continued hearing. = (501/CFH)

In order to make a determination as to the compensation and in light of counsels' alleged agreement to abate fees in order to ensure that the beneficiary received at least $500,000, the Court needs information regarding the costs of winding up the receivership, i.e. reserve. Previously the court issued a tentative ruling requesting that everyone involved submit their fees and costs claims. The Court continued the hearing with instructions to do so. The Court received several claims from parties and their counsel. No claim was received from the receiver and his counsel. The Court received a response from the Receiver indicating that no fees are sought "at this time." The Court interprets this statement to mean that receiver and receiver's counsel are not seeking any fees in this action because the Court ordered that claims for fees incurred (and estimated, if applicable) are to be submitted in advance of this continued hearing. = (501/CFH)

rulings.Case Title: ANDREW MIRONOV VS. TIN LEE ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for March 27, 2019 line 6. PLAINTIFF ANDREW MIRONOV MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT /Adjudication Motion for Summary Judgment is Denied. Triable issues of fact as to the change of terms of tenancy. Motion for Summary Adjudication is GRANTED in part and denied in part. Motion is granted as follows: (1) affirmative defenses 12, 21, 26, 31, 38, 8, 17, 33, 34, and 35 - no opposition; (2) affirmative defenses 1, 2,6, 3, 4, 25, 29, 9, 20, 23, and 24 - not affirmative defenses; per Defendant they are special denials; (3) affirmative defenses 7, 11, 14, 32, 37, 13, 15, 16, 18, 19, 22, 36, and 28 as Defendants fails to cite any authority for the propositions on which these defenses are based, e.g. (a) that the is landlord discriminating against a tenant by selecting a legal ground for eviction that does not afford a defense to eviction; (b)that alleged retaliatory acts (e.g. demands to limit the number of occupants in a unit) in response to tenant's refusal of a buy-out offer is a defense to a pending Ellis Act; (c) that owner's intent to owner-occupy is inconsistent with the intent to go out of residential rental business; etc. Motion for Summary Adjudication is DENIED as to affirmative defenses 5, 10, 27 and 30. Triable issues of fact regarding change of terms of tenancy. =(501/CFH)

rulings.Case Title: ANDREW MIRONOV VS. TIN LEE ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for March 27, 2019 line 6. DEFENDANT KATIE LEE, JULIE LEE, CANDIE LEE MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT DENIED. As to the first argument, Defendants failed to shift their burden. There is no authority cited for the proposition that plaintiff lacks bona fide intent to go out of the residential renal business because he never intended to be a landlord in the first place. As to the second argument regarding change of terms and decreased services (including retaliation) there are multiple triable issues of fact regarding the existence of the alleged terms and change of such terms. Prevailing party to prepare an order in accordance with this ruling and in compliance with CCP 437c(h). = (501/CFH)
